core_story.summary: Being a crucial part of digital avatar generation, listening head generation has many po- tential applications, such as movie production, online cus- tomer service, and human-computer interaction. However, for listening head generation, the listener feedback may be related to an even longer duration of the speaker’s content. Abstract Listening head generation aims to synthesize non-verbal responsive listening head videos that naturally react to a certain speaker, for which, both realistic head movements, expressive facial expressions, and high visual qualities are expected. In this work, we propose a novel listening head generation method that harnesses the generative capabilities of the dif- fusion model for both motion generation and high-quality rendering. We conduct compre- hensive experiments and obtain superior performance in terms of both visual quality and motion expressiveness com- pared with existing methods.

Listening head generation aims to generate responsive lis-
tener videos that react to the corresponding speaker [46].
Unlike talking head videos, the listener’s response mainly
consists of non-verbal behaviors, such as nodding, smil-

tomer service, and human-computer interaction.

actions, the audience

problem_expression: Being a crucial part of digital avatar generation, listening head generation has many po- tential applications, such as movie production, online cus- tomer service, and human-computer interaction.
limitation_expression: Previous approaches typically follow a two-stage pipeline that first generates intermediate 3D motion signals such as 3DMM coefficients, and then synthesizes the videos by deterministic rendering, suffering from limited motion expressiveness and low visual quality ( e.g.
Though promising, they still fall short of generating expressive and natural lis- tening head motions, as well as photorealistic rendering.
However, for listening head generation, the listener feedback may be related to an even longer duration of the speaker’s content.

method_expression: In this work, we propose a novel listening head generation method that harnesses the generative capabilities of the dif- fusion model for both motion generation and high-quality rendering.

Crucially, we propose an effective hybrid motion modeling module that addresses training difficulties caused by the scarcity of listening head data while preserving the intricate details that may be lost in explicit motion repre- sentations.
Our method enables high- fidelity video generation with 512×512 resolution and de- livers vivid listener motion feedback.
In this work, we propose a novel listening head gener- ation framework that enables both highly expressive head motions and photorealistic rendering.
To address these challenges, we propose a novel hybrid motion modeling method, which consists of an explicit and explainable 3D motion representation and a learnable im- plicit latent representation.
To facilitate controlla- bility, we propose distinct control strategies considering the different intrinsic natures of head poses and facial expres- sions.

Our contributions are as follows, • We propose a novel diffusion-based method for motion generation and visual rendering, resulting in realistic and vivid listening head generation. • We introduce an effective hybrid motion modeling mod- ule that overcomes training challenges while enhancing both motion dynamics and visual quality. • We conduct comprehensive experiments and significantly improve listening head generation in both visual quality and motion quality compared with existing methods.

Conclusion In this paper, we propose a diffusion-based listening head generation framework that generates photorealistic listen- ing head videos up to 512×512 resolution, with highly expressive motions and fine facial details.

Comprehensive experiments on Realtalk and ViCo dataset demonstrate that our framework achieves superior results in terms of visual quality and mo- tion expressiveness compared with several state-of-the-art methods.

raw_sections.abstract:
Abstract
Listening head generation aims to synthesize non-verbal
responsive listening head videos that naturally react to a
certain speaker, for which, both realistic head movements,
expressive facial expressions, and high visual qualities are
expected. Previous approaches typically follow a two-stage
pipeline that first generates intermediate 3D motion signals
such as 3DMM coefficients, and then synthesizes the videos
by deterministic rendering, suffering from limited motion
expressiveness and low visual quality ( e.g. 256×256). In
this work, we propose a novel listening head generation
method that harnesses the generative capabilities of the dif-
fusion model for both motion generation and high-quality
rendering. Crucially, we propose an effective hybrid motion
modeling module that addresses training difficulties caused
by the scarcity of listening head data while preserving the
intricate details that may be lost in explicit motion repre-
sentations. We further develop a tailored control guidance
for head pose and facial expression, by integrating their in-
trinsic motion characteristics. Our method enables high-
fidelity video generation with 512×512 resolution and de-
livers vivid listener motion feedback. We conduct compre-
hensive experiments and obtain superior performance in
terms of both visual quality and motion expressiveness com-
pared with existing methods.
raw_sections.introduction:
1. Introduction
Listening head generation aims to generate responsive lis-
tener videos that react to the corresponding speaker [46].
Unlike talking head videos, the listener’s response mainly
consists of non-verbal behaviors, such as nodding, smil-
*Equal contribution
† Corresponding authors
‡ This work was done during Yinuo Wang’s internship at Ant Group
ing, frowning, head shaking, etc. The realism and vividness
of the listener feedback are vital to the success of speaker
and listener communication. Being a crucial part of digital
avatar generation, listening head generation has many po-
tential applications, such as movie production, online cus-
tomer service, and human-computer interaction.
While most works focus on speaker-centric talking head
generation [16, 17, 26–28, 37, 44], only a few study lis-
tening head generation [21, 24, 25, 46]. It is a non-trivial
problem as given a certain speaker video, there can be many
reasonable listener reactions. Such a one-to-many mapping
problem makes it challenging for the model to learn to gen-
erate proper yet diverse listener motions. In addition, since
the generated listening head mostly contains non-verbal re-
actions, the audience may focus more on the listeners’ facial
expressions, requiring that the generated reaction videos be
high-fidelity to manifest detailed and subtle expressions.
The state-of-the-art works of listening head generation
follow a two-stage design that first generates intermediate
listener motion signals and then synthesizes the RGB videos
with post-rendering [20, 21, 35, 46]. For motion generation,
explicit 3D motion signals, such as 3DMM [3] coefficients
are used as motion descriptions. Existing works developed
different models to generate the listener’s 3D motion coeffi-
cients, including LSTM [46], VQ-V AE [35], and diffusion-
based models [20, 21]. As for the video rendering, PIRen-
derer [29] is used to generate the RGB frames conditioned
on the generated 3DMM coefficients. Though promising,
they still fall short of generating expressive and natural lis-
tening head motions, as well as photorealistic rendering.
For instance, 1) the intermediate parametric 3D motion sig-
nals, such as the 3DMM expression coefficients, are char-
acterized in a low-dimensional generalizable subspace and
lack facial details and subtle expressions, which limits the
richness and naturalness of the learned facial expressions, 2)
while focusing more on motion generation, the determinis-
This CVPR paper is the Open Access version, provided by the Computer Vision Foundation.
Except for this watermark, it is identical to the accepted version;
the final published version of the proceedings is available on IEEE Xplore.
15885

tic rendering used in existing works is of low visual quality,
suffering from low resolution (e.g. 256×256), identity in-
consistency, improper head motions, and facial artifacts.
In this work, we propose a novel listening head gener-
ation framework that enables both highly expressive head
motions and photorealistic rendering. Our motivation is to
fully leverage the generative ability of the diffusion model
for both motion generation and video synthesis. Recently,
the latent diffusion model (LDM) [30] has shown supe-
rior ability in high-resolution and vivid video generation,
largely promoting the speaker-centric talking head genera-
tion performance [7, 34, 41]. Nevertheless, it is not feasi-
ble to directly apply these methods to listening head gen-
eration. Firstly, they require hundreds of hours of talk-
ing head videos for training the audio-to-visual correspon-
dence, which does not apply to listening head generation,
where only a few hours of training data are available. Sec-
ondly, due to the high computational and memory cost,
these methods can only model the context information in
a small window size (e.g. 16 frames in [34]). This is accept-
able for talking head generation since talking heads have a
strong correlation with the audio. However, for listening
head generation, the listener feedback may be related to an
even longer duration of the speaker’s content. To generate
a reasonable reaction, it may require long-term modeling
of audio conditions and motion. Based on these, it is non-
trivial to unleash the diffusion generative ability for listen-
ing head generation.
To address these challenges, we propose a novel hybrid
motion modeling method, which consists of an explicit and
explainable 3D motion representation and a learnable im-
plicit latent representation. Specifically, instead of utiliz-
ing the LDM-based rendering to generate listening video
directly from the speaker’s input, we first learn explicit
3D motion signals by a diffusion-based motion generation
module. The explicit motions are later used as guiding con-
ditions to train the rendering module. To facilitate controlla-
bility, we propose distinct control strategies considering the
different intrinsic natures of head poses and facial expres-
sions. The explicit motion generation module is expected
to generate feasible and diverse listener motions but may
lack facial details, as analyzed above. We further develop
an implicit motion refinement module to complement the
potentially missing facial details. With these designs, we
successfully train the LDM-based rendering model on the
small-scale listening dataset, enabling high-fidelity render-
ing (e.g. 512×512) and vivid and expressive listener motion
generation. Compared with the existing methods, we signif-
icantly boost listening head generation performance in both
visual and motion quality.
The hybrid motion design brings several advantages, 1)
the explicit motion generation module is lightweight and
takes much less computational resources than directly mod-
eling audio-to-visual correspondence, making it possible to
learn the long-term relations between speaker and listener,
which is essential for listener motion modeling. 2) The
learning of the hybrid motion representation is much easier
than the pure latent motion modeling, especially on limited
training data. In addition, with the intermediate explicit rep-
resentation, we can better utilize talking videos to pre-train
the rendering module. We conduct thorough ablation stud-
ies and verify the necessity of the hybrid motion modeling.
Our contributions are as follows,
• We propose a novel diffusion-based method for motion
generation and visual rendering, resulting in realistic and
vivid listening head generation.
• We introduce an effective hybrid motion modeling mod-
ule that overcomes training challenges while enhancing
both motion dynamics and visual quality.
• We conduct comprehensive experiments and significantly
improve listening head generation in both visual quality
and motion quality compared with existing methods.
raw_sections.method:
3. Method
Given a single listener portrait image and a speaker’s audio
and video, we aim to generate a non-verbal responsive lis-
tening head video that synchronizes with the speaker, with
detailed facial expressions and natural head movements.
Our overall framework is shown in Fig. 1.
raw_sections.conclusion:
5. Conclusion
In this paper, we propose a diffusion-based listening head
generation framework that generates photorealistic listen-
ing head videos up to 512×512 resolution, with highly
expressive motions and fine facial details. A hybrid motion
modeling method that consists of an explicit motion gener-
ation module, a specially designed motion control strategy
and an implicit motion refining process eases the training
of the LDM-based framework, making the learning on a
small scale dataset feasible. Comprehensive experiments on
Realtalk and ViCo dataset demonstrate that our framework
achieves superior results in terms of visual quality and mo-
tion expressiveness compared with several state-of-the-art
methods. And a thorough ablation study proves the effec-
tiveness and necessity of the proposed hybrid motion mod-
eling method. In the future, we will study further to improve
the controllability and the generating speed of our model.
